cases.description: index_num=2 selects the second value in the list
index_num beyond the number of supplied values -> #VALUE!
cases.expected_note: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/choose-function-fc5c184f-cb62-4ec7-a46e-38653b98f5bc -- 'If index_num is less than 1 or greater than the number of the last value in the list, CHOOSE returns the #VALUE! error value.'

cases.description: Non-integer index_num is truncated toward the lowest integer before use, not rounded
cases.expected_note: Same doc as above: 'If index_num is a fraction, it is truncated to the lowest integer before being used.' 2.9 truncates to 2, not rounded to 3

cases.description: CHOOSE can select an entire range as its 'value', which is then fed to SUM
cases.expected_note: index_num=2 selects the B1:B3 range; SUM of 10+20+30=60
